stories.headline: SEC, CFTC unite at Bitcoin 2026 to tease Strategic Reserve and tokenization sandbox
stories.tldr: U.S. regulators showed a unified front at Bitcoin 2026 in Las Vegas, signaling innovation exemptions and a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve announcement within weeks.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A coordinated SEC-CFTC stance plus a Strategic Reserve reveal would remove the single biggest overhang from U.S. crypto markets — and front-run the CLARITY Act Lummis wants finished by August.
the_details: SEC Chair Paul Atkins said innovation exemptions for on-chain tokenization and securitization land in the coming weeks.
White House digital-assets council director Patrick Witt previewed a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve announcement is imminent.
Senator Lummis is pushing the CLARITY Act through Congress with an August target date.

stories.headline: BTC tumbles below $77K as U.S.-Iran talks stall and Brent reclaims $104
stories.tldr: Bitcoin fell to $76,472 and Ethereum to $2,278 Tuesday as stalled Iran ceasefire negotiations and renewed oil pressure hit risk assets ahead of the Fed.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: BTC's correlation with the dollar index has hit -0.90, the most negative since 2022, so any DXY breakout from geopolitical risk now translates almost 1-for-1 into spot weakness.
the_details: BTC opened at $77,368 Tuesday, down 1.6% on the day, then slid to $76,472 by 7:10 a.m. ET.
ETH dropped 2.8% to $2,278 as the Strait of Hormuz remained closed and Brent crude pushed back above $104.
Macro traders are positioning for the third Fed rate decision of 2026 later this week.

stories.headline: April clocks $606M in crypto hacks — worst month since Feb 2025, with Lazarus driving 95%
stories.tldr: April 2026 has already produced $606M in crypto theft across 18 days, eclipsing all of Q1 combined and pushing year-to-date losses past $771M.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: With private-key compromises and human-layer social engineering — not contract bugs — driving the carnage, audits no longer protect treasuries; signing infrastructure and operational security are the new perimeter.
the_details: Lazarus-linked Drift Protocol ($285M, April 1) and Kelp DAO ($292M, April 18) account for ~95% of monthly losses.
April's haul is 3.7x larger than Q1 2026 and the worst single month since February 2025.
Smaller hits include CoW Swap ($1.2M domain hijack), Dango bridge ($410K) and Silo Finance ($392K oracle bug).

stories.headline: Polygon ships sPOL liquid staking ahead of POL hard fork to unlock staked capital
stories.tldr: Polygon launched native liquid staking token sPOL, freeing staked POL for DeFi use, and queued a hard fork to bolster scalability and security.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Native liquid staking turns Polygon's validator stack into productive collateral and gives POL a credible answer to Ethereum's stETH-led DeFi stack — critical for any L2-era TVL relevance.
the_details: sPOL lets stakers redeploy capital into lending, LPs and structured products without unbonding.
An upcoming hard fork is timed to harden the network through the final MATIC-to-POL migration.
DeFi market cap rose 3.3% on the day to $56.2B with $5.05B in 24-hour volume.

stories.headline: Bhutan moves another 250 BTC as 2026 outflows top $240M, stack down 73% from peak
stories.tldr: Arkham flagged Bhutan's sovereign wallet sending 250 BTC on April 13, extending the kingdom's 2026 outflows past $240M and trimming holdings 73% from the October 2024 peak.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A sovereign holder steadily distributing BTC into a $77K market is the kind of slow-bleed supply pressure that resistance levels are made of — and a reminder that not every government accumulator stays an accumulator.
the_details: On-chain analytics firm Arkham tracked the 250 BTC transfer on April 13.
Cumulative 2026 outflows from Bhutan's wallets now exceed $240 million.
The country's stack has fallen 73% from its October 2024 peak.
